title: Cosmos Mini Sudoku — fill a 6x6 grid so every row, column and 2x3 region has 1-6
summary: A real, self-contained mini sudoku puzzle in a single HTML file with zero dependencies. The board is a 6x6 grid divided into six 2x3 regions; the goal is to fill every empty cell so each row, each column and each region contains the digits 1 through 6 exactly once. A fresh seeded puzzle is generated each game with a unique solution and a set of locked given clues; tap a cell then a number on the 1-6 pad (or press number keys) to fill it, conflicting entries flash in nebula pink, and you win the instant the grid is correctly completed. Playable with mouse or touch, with R for a new puzzle. It also ships a deterministic self-playing attract demo for capture: a precomputed solution fills the empty cells one by one, the current target cell pulsing, rendered as a pure function of time via window.__seek(t).
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html. CONTROLS: tap/click an empty (non-given) cell to select it, then tap a digit on the 1-6 number pad below the board, or press keys 1-6; tapping the same digit again or pressing 0/Backspace clears the cell; the R key or the New Puzzle button starts a fresh seeded puzzle. GOAL: fill all empty cells so every row, every column and every 2x3 region contains 1-6 once each; given clues are locked and marked with a small dot, conflicting digits flash pink, and the round is won the moment the board is correctly solved (FILLED/LEFT counters track progress). KEY TECHNIQUES: a backtracking solver (solve) seeded by mulberry32 builds a full valid grid, then a deterministic dig removes cells down to a clue count to make the puzzle; valid() checks row, column and 2x3 region constraints and is reused for live conflict highlighting. The AUTO attract demo is built once from a fixed seed: the empty cells are ordered row-major and a precomputed solution fills them one at a time, with simTo-style time math (k = floor(time/STEP_MS)) making the visible board a pure function of time, the current target cell pulsing and partially drawn, and START_OFFSET placing frame 0 mid-fill over a clue-populated board. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: STEP_MS fill speed, START_OFFSET, PUZSEED/CLUES for the demo puzzle, the NUMCOL digit palette, the live clue count range, LOOP.
